talk_metadata.talk_title: Evolution of PARP inhibitors and lessons for synthetic lethality
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't fully understand why some patients escaped
Skipped OS data entirely, presented only PFS

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Incomplete PARP trapping leading to sub‑optimal tumor regression
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: more than 90% of PARP inhibitor‑resistant models ... will actually reactivate HR function, and you'll see RAD51 foci
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: some patients responded despite low dose, but overall cohort benefit was modest
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: higher dose reductions and interruptions were needed for many patients due to toxicity

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Phase III olaparib maintenance trial in first‑line ovarian cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all HRD patients without stratifying by functional HRD status (e.g., RAD51 foci)
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Functional HRD assay (RAD51 foci) to exclude HR‑proficient tumors
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential underpowering of sub‑group analyses for different BRCA mutation types

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Broad PARP trapping activity varies between agents, leading to differing therapeutic windows
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Dose reductions due to toxicity dilute signal and may limit long‑term efficacy

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed RAD51 foci data indicating HR re‑activation in resistant tumors, then concluded that PARP inhibitors remain broadly effective as monotherapy

crispro_opportunity.description: Functional HRD assays (e.g., RAD51 foci, STRIDE) could better select patients who will benefit from high‑trap PARP inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: RAD51 foci expression indicating reactivation of HR in non‑responders; >90% of resistant models show RAD51 foci.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate functional HRD signatures with genomic data to predict PARP trapping dependency.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: retrieve HRD biomarker data from olaparib maintenance trials
PubMed: studies linking RAD51 foci to PARP inhibitor response

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine high‑trap PARP inhibitors with agents that prevent HR re‑activation (e.g., POLQ inhibitors) to overcome resistance.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Resistant clones lost ability to trap PARP; loss of trapping correlated with resistance.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synthetic lethal networks to prioritize POLQ as a partner for PARP inhibition.
external_validation_needed: Preclinical data on POLQ inhibition synergy with PARP trapping

cited_competitors.name: Rucaparib
cited_competitors.context: Compared for trapping potency and toxicity profile

cited_competitors.name: Talazoparib
cited_competitors.context: Highlighted as a strong trap with narrow therapeutic window

cited_competitors.name: Niraparib
cited_competitors.context: Noted for off‑target toxicities (hypertension, LFT elevations)

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal PARP inhibition threshold (time above IC95) needed for durable responses?
Can functional HRD assays replace static genomic scar assays in trial eligibility?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Targeting ARID1A/ARID1B paralogs and SMARCA2/SMARCA4 synthetic lethality
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: ARID1B is probably one of the most challenging targets that I've worked on in my career
Skipped discussion of any in‑vivo efficacy data for ARID1B degraders

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target neo‑substrate activity of cereblon degraders not fully explored in primary cells
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Hook effect observed with cereblon‑based degrader at higher concentrations

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: ARID1B selective binders converted to degraders
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Selectivity relies on binder chemistry; any loss of selectivity could lead to ARID1A degradation, reducing therapeutic index
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Lack of demonstrated in‑vivo tumor regression data limits confidence in advancing to clinic

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: SMARCA2 inhibitor (LY4050784) in collaboration with Lilly
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Efficacy appears limited to BRG1‑mutant tumors; narrow patient population
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on combination with PD‑1 blockade adds complexity to trial design

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker emphasized high selectivity of ARID1B degraders (no ARID1A degradation) yet admitted a hook effect and potential off‑target neo‑substrates, which could compromise selectivity

key_data_gaps: No in‑vivo efficacy or safety data for ARID1B degraders
Limited pharmacokinetic data for SMARCA2 inhibitor

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to redesign ARID1B binders to eliminate hook effect and improve degradation kinetics.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Hook effect observed with cereblon‑based molecule; need for better potency/selectivity.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Computational protein‑ligand modeling can predict off‑target interactions and guide redesign.
external_validation_needed: Structural data of ARID1B‑cereblon ternary complex
Cellular degradation assays in multiple tumor models

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify synergistic partners for SMARCA2 inhibition (e.g., ATR inhibitors) using synthetic lethal network analysis.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: SMARCA2 inhibition combined with PD‑1 blockade showed complete responses in mouse model.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can map dependencies in BRG1‑deficient tumors to prioritize rational combos.
external_validation_needed: Preclinical combination studies of SMARCA2 inhibitor with ATR or DNA‑PK inhibitors

cited_competitors.name: VHL and cereblon E3 ligase platforms
cited_competitors.context: Compared for potency and selectivity in degrader development

unresolved_questions: Will ARID1B degraders achieve sufficient tumor exposure and on‑target activity in vivo?
What biomarkers will predict response to SMARCA2 inhibition beyond BRG1 loss?
